source: Cederman & Pengl, ETH Zürich (2019). Global Conflict Trends and their Consequences.
dataset: Uppsala Conflict Data Program (UCDP), Ethnic Power Relations (EPR)

conflict_wave_model.description: Three historical waves of civil conflict since WWII (Walter 2017 / Cederman-Pengl synthesis)

waves.label: Cold War Ideological Wave
waves.driver: Superpower proxy funding of ideological insurgencies (Marxist vs capitalist)
waves.prediction_signal: Decline after Soviet collapse; replaced by ethnic mobilization

waves.label: Post-Cold War Ethnic Wave
waves.driver: Ethnic group exclusion, power vacuum after Soviet dissolution, decolonization legacies
waves.prediction_signal: Declined via Gurr accommodation regime: inclusion, autonomy, power sharing, UN peacekeeping

waves.label: Islamist Transnational Wave
waves.driver: Authoritarian exclusion, failed Arab nationalism, social media radicalization, Saudi Salafist funding
waves.prediction_signal: IS caliphate collapse 2019 may reduce peak; but ideology diffuses transnationally

driver_patterns.name: Ethnic Group Political Exclusion
driver_patterns.mechanism: Ethnic groups excluded from executive power are 2–4x more likely to rebel (EPR dataset, Cederman 2013)
driver_patterns.prevention: Regional autonomy, power-sharing coalitions, end of formal discrimination

driver_patterns.name: Authoritarian Repression of Non-violent Opposition
driver_patterns.mechanism: States blocking peaceful participation leave populations with 'no other way out' (Goodwin 2001). Creates grievance base for extremist recruitment.
driver_patterns.prevention: External pressure for inclusive governance, conditional aid

driver_patterns.name: Digital Radicalization & Transnational Recruitment
driver_patterns.mechanism: Social media platforms enable extremist ideology diffusion across borders. Islamophobic content increases pro-ISIS sentiment (Mitts 2019). Islamic State exploited these platforms for transnational recruitment.
indicators: High social media penetration in conflict zones
State censorship attempts with low efficacy
driver_patterns.prevention: Platform content moderation, counter-narrative campaigns, reducing anti-Muslim discrimination

driver_patterns.name: Economic Inequality & Conflict Trap
driver_patterns.mechanism: Civil war reduces GDP per capita by 2.2%/year (Collier 1999). 15-year conflict → 30% income loss. Creates poverty trap that increases conflict recurrence probability.
driver_patterns.prevention: SDG 8 (economic growth), post-conflict reconstruction investment, SDG 1 (poverty)

driver_patterns.name: Strategic Resource Competition
driver_patterns.mechanism: Control over critical resources (oil, minerals, water) provides both motive and funding for armed groups. Resource curse amplifies corruption and ethnic patronage systems.
driver_patterns.prevention: Revenue transparency, Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative (EITI)

driver_patterns.name: Nuclear Proliferation & Arms Race Risk
driver_patterns.mechanism: NPT erosion increases first-use risk. India-Pakistan geographic proximity undermines nuclear crisis stability. Iran/North Korea arsenals create preemptive action incentives for regional powers.
driver_patterns.prevention: Arms control treaties, diplomatic de-escalation, nuclear-free zones

regional_risk_profiles.conflict_intensity_trend: HIGH — peaked 2015-2016, declining but above pre-2011 baseline
regional_risk_profiles.primary_driver: Authoritarian exclusion + ethnic/sectarian fragmentation + external meddling
regional_risk_profiles.future_scenario: Negative equilibrium trap: inequality + conflict + poor economic growth + external powers supporting incumbents

regional_risk_profiles.conflict_intensity_trend: RISING — IS/AQ affiliates expanding post-caliphate collapse
regional_risk_profiles.primary_driver: Non-state conflict surge + IS/al-Qaeda franchise expansion + ethnic communal violence
regional_risk_profiles.future_scenario: Sahel ungoverned space expands; Boko Haram, JNIM, IS-Sahel threaten Nigeria, Niger, Mali, Cameroon

regional_risk_profiles.primary_driver: India-Pakistan nuclear risk + Afghanistan post-NATO instability + religious nationalism
regional_risk_profiles.future_scenario: Pakistan-India conventional escalation risk; Afghanistan Taliban consolidation enables IS-K expansion

regional_risk_profiles.conflict_intensity_trend: PERSISTENT — DRC conflict trap; resource conflict
regional_risk_profiles.primary_driver: Ethnic fractionalization + resource competition (cobalt, coltan) + weak state capacity
regional_risk_profiles.future_scenario: Cobalt demand for EV transition escalates resource conflict; militia proliferation continues

regional_risk_profiles.primary_driver: Territorial revisionism + nuclear doctrine shift + populist ethno-nationalism
regional_risk_profiles.future_scenario: NPT norm erosion; populist accommodation regime erosion in EU threatens minority rights

regional_risk_profiles.primary_driver: US-China trade war + South China Sea territorial competition + Taiwan strait
regional_risk_profiles.future_scenario: Gray-zone operations (cable cutting, cyber, economic coercion) below threshold of armed conflict

conflict_prevention_framework.name: Gurr Accommodation Regime (updated Cederman 2017)
conflict_prevention_framework.warning: Post-2015 populist ethno-nationalism in Europe/USA threatens this regime — could trigger ethnic conflict resurgence

predictive_rules.name: Ethnic Exclusion → Conflict Onset
predictive_rules.logic: IF ethnic group population share > 15% AND political exclusion status = 'Discriminated' OR 'Powerless' THEN conflict_onset_probability += 35%

predictive_rules.name: Authoritarian + High Youth Unemployment → Islamist Recruitment
predictive_rules.logic: IF Polity IV < -3 AND youth unemployment > 25% AND Muslim majority country THEN non-state_conflict_risk += 40%

predictive_rules.name: Post-Conflict Poverty Trap
predictive_rules.logic: IF conflict_duration > 5 years AND GDP_loss > 20% THEN conflict_recurrence_within_10yr = 45%

predictive_rules.name: Resource Curse Amplification
predictive_rules.logic: IF primary commodity exports > 25% GDP AND ethnic fractionalization > 0.5 THEN conflict_intensity_multiplier = 1.8x

predictive_rules.name: Nuclear Instability Trigger
predictive_rules.logic: IF nuclear_state AND conventional_military_loss_imminent THEN first_use_consideration_risk = HIGH
predictive_rules.evidence: Cederman & Pengl 2019 scenario analysis; India-Pakistan geographic proximity

predictive_rules.name: Accommodation Regime Erosion
predictive_rules.logic: IF populist_ethno-nationalist government = TRUE AND minority rights protections declining = TRUE THEN ethnic_conflict_recurrence_risk += 25%
predictive_rules.evidence: Cederman & Pengl 2019 policy conclusions; democratic backsliding post-2015
